en_title: Should We Fear Consoles Going All-Digital, or Does the PC Status Quo Prove It Works?
cn_title: 主机全面数字化值得担忧吗？PC现状给出哪些启示
subtitle: 光盘退场之后
opus_summary: PC游戏早已全面数字化，Steam的成功证明无光盘生态可以便利而繁荣；但PlayStation是由索尼完全掌控的封闭平台，取消实体盘后，价格、数字所有权、下架风险和游戏保存仍需警惕。

paragraphs.en: Game discs have taken a one-two punch over the last couple of weeks: first Rockstar’s announcement that the biggest game launch of the decade , then Sony laying out plans to stop making discs for PlayStation consoles by January 2028 . Backlash has been fierce, but it’s hard to imagine either company u-turning – the discless future is on its way, whether we like it or not.
paragraphs.cn: 过去几周，游戏光盘接连遭受两记重击：先是Rockstar宣布这款十年来最重磅的游戏将不发行实体版，随后索尼又计划在2028年1月前停止为PlayStation主机制作光盘。反对声十分激烈，但很难想象两家公司会回头——无论我们是否喜欢，无光盘的未来都正在到来。

paragraphs.en: For gamers on PC, however, that future is already the present. A desktop has long been my main gaming machine, and frankly I can’t remember the last time I bought a disc – it’s been a while since I’ve even had a drive to slot one into.
paragraphs.cn: 然而对PC玩家来说，这个未来早已成为现实。台式机长期以来都是我的主要游戏平台；坦白说，我已经记不起上次购买光盘是什么时候，甚至已经很多年没有能放入光盘的驱动器了。

paragraphs.en: For over a decade, discless has been the blanket status quo on PC. While that has come with problems, overall the platform is thriving more than ever, with most players now deeply loyal to dominant digital storefront Steam.
paragraphs.cn: 十多年来，无光盘一直是PC平台的普遍常态。虽然它也带来问题，但PC整体比以往任何时候都更繁荣，多数玩家如今对占据主导地位的数字商店Steam相当忠诚。

paragraphs.en: Does that mean a digital future for consoles is all going to be a-ok? Well… maybe. Let’s look back at the history of discless gaming on PC – and what it tells us about where things will go from here.
paragraphs.cn: 这是否意味着主机的数字化未来一定安然无恙？也许吧。让我们回顾PC无光盘游戏的历史，看看它对未来走向有何启示。

paragraphs.en: When Steam first launched in 2003, it was not warmly received. Many players saw it as unnecessary digital rights management (DRM), questioned the purpose of Valve’s new digital storefront, and resisted the idea of ongoing patches and updates to their games.
paragraphs.cn: Steam于2003年刚上线时并不受欢迎。许多玩家认为它是不必要的数字版权管理（DRM），质疑Valve新数字商店存在的意义，也抗拒游戏持续接受补丁和更新的理念。

paragraphs.en: At the time, that resistance was understandable. Many players were still on dial-up connections, and the idea of being always online was still a long way off. On top of that, the user experience of launch-era Steam was clunky – an awkward hurdle to jump to be part of an idea that felt half-formed.
paragraphs.cn: 这种抵触在当时可以理解。许多玩家仍使用拨号上网，持续联网还是遥远概念；而且初版Steam的体验十分笨拙，用户必须跨过一道难用的门槛，才能加入一个看起来尚未成形的构想。

paragraphs.en: But if you wanted Half-Life 2, and then Portal and Team Fortress 2, you had to get used to it – what were you going to do, not play some of the best PC games ever made? That gave it a foothold, and before long it grew on us.
paragraphs.cn: 但如果你想玩《半衰期2》，以及后来的《传送门》和《军团要塞2》，就必须习惯Steam——难道要放弃这些史上最优秀的PC游戏吗？这让Steam站稳脚跟，没过多久，我们也逐渐接受了它。

paragraphs.en: The addition of 3rd party games to the storefront – starting with the oft-forgotten Rag Doll Kung Fu – cemented the idea that it could be something bigger than just a place to download Valve games. The Steam app itself became increasingly slick and functional. And around it, the world caught up, with both faster internet speeds and bigger hard drives becoming more and more accessible.
paragraphs.cn: 商店从常被遗忘的《Rag Doll Kung Fu》开始引入第三方游戏，进一步证明Steam可以不只是下载Valve游戏的地方。Steam应用本身也愈发流畅实用；与此同时，外部条件追赶上来，更快的网速和更大容量的硬盘越来越普及。

paragraphs.en: At the same time, those factors were contributing to the rise of online PC game piracy – a looming (if arguably overstated) threat that, combined with the second-hand market, made publishers increasingly wary of physical discs. Methods were deployed to encourage players not to share or copy discs, including one-time-use codes unlocking exclusive content and online multiplayer modes in singleplayer games. But none were as effective as simply selling games digitally, forever bound to one person’s account.
paragraphs.cn: 与此同时，这些因素也推动了PC游戏网络盗版的兴起。这一潜在威胁或许被夸大，但与二手市场叠加后，仍让发行商越来越警惕实体光盘。厂商曾采用多种方式阻止玩家分享或复制光盘，包括用一次性代码解锁独家内容，或解锁单机游戏的在线多人模式；但都不如直接销售永久绑定个人账号的数字游戏有效。

paragraphs.en: For consumers, Steam increasingly became less an obstruction and more a convenience – a one-stop shop for all your games, with regular sales and offers that physical stores couldn’t match encouraging loyalty. As players increasingly chose digital purchases over physical, publishers figured they were better off pulling back on physical discs and investing in their own attempts at digital storefronts instead (to varying degrees of success).
paragraphs.cn: 对消费者而言，Steam越来越不像障碍，而更像便利工具：所有游戏都能一站购齐，频繁促销与实体店无法匹敌的优惠也培养了忠诚度。随着玩家日益偏向数字购买，发行商认为缩减实体光盘、转而投资自家数字商店更划算，尽管各家的成果有好有坏。

paragraphs.en: By the 2010s, physical game discs for PC were already practically unheard of, and as the same move to digital and streaming solutions happened across software, music, film, and TV, soon the final nail was hammered into the coffin: PCs stopped shipping with disc drives at all. The CD-ROM was officially dead.
paragraphs.cn: 到了2010年代，PC实体游戏光盘几乎已经绝迹。软件、音乐、电影和电视也纷纷转向数字与流媒体方案，棺材上的最后一颗钉子很快落下：PC出厂时彻底不再配备光驱，CD-ROM正式死亡。

paragraphs.en: So, how’s that going now? Well, not too bad for the most part. These days, those playing primarily on PC hardly miss game discs at all – even in their most nostalgic moments sparing more of a thought for cloth maps and chunky manuals than the CD-ROMs that came with them. In fact, most have become so accustomed to the modern convenience of Steam that they resent any other method or platform for purchasing games.
paragraphs.cn: 如今情况如何？总体而言并不差。现在以PC为主要平台的玩家几乎不会怀念游戏光盘；即使最念旧时，想到的也更多是布质地图和厚重说明书，而非随附的CD-ROM。事实上，多数人已经习惯Steam带来的现代便利，反而会排斥其他购买方式或平台。

paragraphs.en: We’ve willingly given up physical media and the benefits that do come with it, trading second hand markets and game sharing for 75% off sales and enormous backlogs.
paragraphs.cn: 我们主动放弃了实体媒介及其好处，用二手市场和游戏分享换来了75%折扣，以及堆积如山的未玩游戏。

paragraphs.en: A decade from now, an all-digital world on PlayStation and other consoles will probably be just as accepted, whether that seems objectionable now or not. But, crucially, PC and consoles are not equivalent platforms – and if Sony doesn’t act responsibly from here, the downsides could be a lot more evident.
paragraphs.cn: 十年后，无论现在看来多么难以接受，PlayStation和其他主机的全数字世界大概也会被普遍接受。但关键在于，PC与主机并非同类平台；如果索尼今后不能负责任地行事，弊端可能会明显得多。

paragraphs.en: The key difference is that PC gaming enjoys the rule of a relatively benevolent dictator. Valve isn’t perfect but it’s a private company, not beholden to shareholders, and its driving motivation seems to be a genuine enthusiasm for gaming and tech as much as profit. Even its most unpopular moves rarely seem cynical or ruthless, and most players are able to broadly trust that Steam will stick around for the foreseeable future as a reliable and user-friendly platform.
paragraphs.cn: 关键区别在于，PC游戏由一位相对仁慈的独裁者统治。Valve并不完美，但它是一家非上市公司，无须向股东负责；推动它前进的动力似乎既有利润，也有对游戏与科技的真诚热情。即使最不受欢迎的举措也很少显得玩世不恭或冷酷，因此多数玩家大体相信，Steam在可预见的未来仍会是可靠且友好的平台。

paragraphs.en: And if it ever does go off the rails, we have other options. Valve enjoys a near-monopoly, but competition still exists and unhappy customers are free to shop elsewhere, including with options like GOG that offer totally DRM-free downloads.
paragraphs.cn: 即便它有一天偏离正轨，我们也有其他选择。Valve虽接近垄断，但竞争仍然存在；不满的消费者可以自由转向别处，包括提供完全无DRM下载的GOG。

paragraphs.en: PlayStation, like all the major consoles, is a closed ecosystem. Sony controls it all directly – and as a massive, publicly-traded company, its motivations are not necessarily customer-friendly. It has competition, but only externally to the platform – you’re free to go and play on an Xbox or a Switch instead, but your PlayStation can only (legally) play games that Sony sells you, on its terms.
paragraphs.cn: PlayStation和所有主流主机一样，是封闭生态。索尼直接控制其中一切；作为一家大型上市公司，它的动机不一定对消费者友好。竞争只存在于平台外部：你可以改玩XBOX或Switch，但PlayStation只能合法运行索尼按自身条件卖给你的游戏。

paragraphs.en: As that grip has grown tighter and tighter, physical discs have acted as one little backdoor in the system. Sony has final say on what is playable on their console, but it can’t control the second-hand market–-if you don’t like its prices, you’re at least free to see if GameStop, or indeed your cousin Greg who’s been clearing out his garage, can make you a better offer.
paragraphs.cn: 随着控制不断收紧，实体光盘一直像系统里的一道小后门。索尼对主机能玩什么拥有最终决定权，却无法控制二手市场。如果不喜欢官方价格，你至少可以看看GameStop，或者正在清理车库的表哥Greg，能否给出更好的报价。

paragraphs.en: Equally, while it can freely change games, remove them, or opt not to make them backwards-compatible, it can’t take your physical discs away from you – and thus you retain some kind of genuine ownership, rather than just a digital license.
paragraphs.cn: 同样，索尼可以自由修改或下架游戏，也可以不提供向后兼容，但无法夺走你的实体光盘。因此你仍保有某种真正的所有权，而不只是一张数字许可证。

paragraphs.en: The phasing out of discs closes the backdoor for good. Outside of illegal console modding and piracy, Sony’s law becomes absolute. So how bad or tolerable the situation becomes depends entirely on how responsibly it acts.
paragraphs.cn: 逐步淘汰光盘会永久关闭这道后门。除非法改机和盗版之外，索尼的规则将成为绝对命令。因此，局面究竟会恶劣还是尚可忍受，完全取决于它是否负责任。

paragraphs.en: The first question is price – with no second-hand market, is Sony free to crank up the cost of its games with impunity? I think this is the issue to worry least about – while a creeping up of the price of launch day triple-A games is at this point inevitable, overall digital distribution has only led to deeper discounts. The PlayStation store’s track record on sales and game pricing is pretty normal, roughly just staying in step with its competition on other consoles.
paragraphs.cn: 第一个问题是价格：没有二手市场后，索尼是否可以毫无顾忌地抬高游戏价格？我认为这是最不必担忧的一点。首发3A游戏价格缓慢上涨如今已不可避免，但数字发行总体上只带来了更大折扣。PlayStation商店在促销和定价方面的记录相当正常，基本与其他主机平台的竞争对手保持同步。

paragraphs.en: More concerning is the way that games can be delisted from the store and potentially even taken away from players who originally bought them – especially following recent news of 550 movies deleted from the platform and removed from customer’s libraries. This is where the concept of ownership itself comes into question. If what you have is only a license to a digital product, your rights to that product in perpetuity are shaky.
paragraphs.cn: 更令人担忧的是，游戏可能从商店下架，甚至从原购买者手中被收回。尤其是最近有消息称，平台删除了550部电影，并将其从消费者资料库移除。这让「所有权」本身受到质疑：如果你拥有的只是一项数字产品许可，那么永久享有该产品的权利并不稳固。

paragraphs.en: It should be said, though, that we don’t currently have any prior example of Sony removing a purchased game from anyone’s library – at least, outside of cases like Concord where the game itself closed down and refunds were issued. That doesn’t make the fear unfounded, but it would at least be an unprecedented move.
paragraphs.cn: 不过必须指出，目前还没有索尼从任何人的资料库移除已购游戏的先例，至少《星鸣特攻》这类游戏本身停运并提供退款的情况除外。这并不意味着担忧毫无根据，但真要发生也会是前所未有的举动。

paragraphs.en: The other, perhaps most pressing headache is game preservation. Holding on to the modern history of games is difficult at the best of times in our increasingly online-focused world. PC, at least, is an open enough platform to make keeping old games alive and playable even when they’re no longer for sale possible more often than not (if not always technically legally).
paragraphs.cn: 另一个也许最迫切的问题是游戏保存。在日益以网络为中心的世界里，即使条件最好时，保存现代游戏历史也并不容易。PC至少足够开放，使停售老游戏在多数情况下仍能存续并运行，尽管技术上未必始终合法。

paragraphs.en: With PlayStation’s closed ecosystem, discs have offered one of the few ways to ensure a game is preserved long-term – and in its original form, before updates may have changed it completely. With that option disappearing, the onus is on Sony to take its responsibility to its own history seriously. Backwards compatibility is one part of that puzzle, and PlayStation’s track record there is currently respectable. But there will continue to be games that simply vanish from the service or have fundamental elements altered as licenses expire, and where they’re not available on other platforms, they’ll simply be gone.
paragraphs.cn: 在PlayStation封闭生态中，光盘是少数能确保游戏长期保存的方式之一，而且能保留更新彻底改变游戏之前的原始形态。随着这一选项消失，索尼必须认真承担保护自身历史的责任。向后兼容是拼图的一部分，PlayStation目前在这方面的记录尚可；但仍会有游戏从服务中直接消失，或因授权到期而改变核心元素。如果它们又未登陆其他平台，就会彻底不复存在。

paragraphs.en: There is, admittedly, something I’ve been leaving unsaid here. The perhaps unfortunate truth of all this is that really this ship already sailed years ago. Simply popping a disc in your console and playing the game right away has not been the experience for a long time. Gaming is now built around the idea that publishers can from day one push major mandatory patches and updates to your system.
paragraphs.cn: 诚然，我一直没有说破一件事：令人遗憾的真相是，这艘船早在多年前就已经启航。把光盘塞进主机便立即开始游戏，早已不是现实体验。如今的游戏体系建立在发行商从第一天起就能向系统推送大型强制补丁和更新的基础上。

paragraphs.en: Disconnect your console from the internet and try playing unpatched, and typically what you get is a deeply compromised version of the game, with major bugs left in on the assumption you would never normally see them. And that’s assuming the game doesn’t withhold major features from offline players, or simply block access completely. In many ways the actual disc has become vestigial to the downloadable experience.
paragraphs.cn: 断开主机网络并尝试游玩未打补丁的版本，通常只能得到严重残缺的游戏；开发者默认玩家平时不会看到它，于是留下重大Bug。前提还是游戏没有对离线玩家扣留关键功能，或干脆完全阻止访问。从许多方面看，实体光盘已成为依附于下载体验的退化器官。

paragraphs.en: Many games are already sold exclusively on digital storefronts, never receiving a disc release at all. That includes many of the games that have been notably delisted from storefronts, meaning discs have been no bastion for those for a while anyway. Meanwhile in other industries, formats like the CD for music or the DVD for film and TV hold on by their fingernails, while consumer focus shifts increasingly to streaming as the go-to solution.
paragraphs.cn: 许多游戏早已只在数字商店销售，从未推出光盘版，其中也包括不少后来从商店下架的知名作品。这意味着对这些游戏而言，光盘早就不是避风港。与此同时，在其他行业，音乐CD和影视DVD等格式勉强维系，消费者的注意力则越来越转向流媒体。

paragraphs.en: In other words, the phasing out of physical discs doesn’t start now; it started about 10 years ago. PC is ahead of the game, but not that far ahead. The fact that the launch of GTA 6 – a truly industry-defining release in financial terms – isn’t bothering with discs is a sign that these major companies are more than comfortable that the status quo has already moved on.
paragraphs.cn: 换言之，实体光盘的淘汰并非现在才开始，而是始于约10年前。PC走在前面，但并没有领先太远。GTA6这款在商业层面真正足以定义行业的作品都不再费心发行光盘，说明大公司非常确信，行业常态早已改变。

paragraphs.en: There’s no standing against the march of progress (especially progress towards more efficient profits for major companies) and even if there was, we’re already years too late to start on this particular front. The focus now has to be on holding companies like Sony to account for their use of this even more consolidated power. When you don’t like what you’re seeing, let them know and vote with your wallet.
paragraphs.cn: 没有人能阻挡进步的脚步，尤其是让大公司更高效获利的那种「进步」；即便可以，我们在这条战线上也已经晚了好几年。现在的重点必须是要求索尼等公司为如何使用这份更加集中的权力负责。看到不喜欢的做法，就明确表达，并用钱包投票。

paragraphs.en: I can’t promise that the future will be bright, but I can at least say that an all-digital platform doesn’t have to be doom and gloom. And hey, if it all goes wrong, you’re very welcome to come join us on PC instead. Er, that is if RAM ever becomes affordable again.
paragraphs.cn: 我无法保证未来一片光明，但至少可以说，全数字平台不必注定阴云密布。要是一切真的走向糟糕，也非常欢迎你来PC平台加入我们——呃，前提是RAM价格还有降回可负担水平的一天。

paragraphs.en: Robin Valentine is a freelancer journalist who combines years of writing experience with his lifelong love of PC gaming. Formerly the editor of PC Gamer magazine (and the dearly departed GamesMaster), he’ll happily devour any RPG, strategy game, or roguelike that stumbles into his path.
paragraphs.cn: Robin Valentine是一名自由记者，将多年写作经验与毕生对PC游戏的热爱结合在一起。他曾任《PC Gamer》杂志及现已停刊的《GamesMaster》编辑，任何RPG、策略游戏或Roguelike只要来到眼前，他都会欣然体验。
